items.description: Egypt were left seething by their narrow and dramatic round-of-16 defeat against Argentina. Coach Hossam Hassan accused the referee of favoring the defending World Cup champions.

items.title: At least 28 dead in China factory fire: state media
items.description: Dozens of fire and rescue personnel were racing to extinguish the blaze that tore through the factory in Jinjiang city, authorities said

items.title: At least 28 people killed as fire engulfs shoe factory in China
items.description: Beijing demands 'all-out' effort to extinguish the blaze, search for survivors and treat the injured.

items.title: International Breweries turns to share reconstruction to clear accumulated losses
items.description: Shares in the brewer accelerated by 10 per cent—the maximum daily gain allowed by the bourse—at market close following the disclosure. The post International Breweries turns to share reconstruction to clear accumulated losses appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Argentina survive Cape Verde scare to reach World Cup last 16
items.description: Cristian Romero's header in injury time secures 3-2 victory over Cape Verde in extra time; African side impressed in hard-fought contest in Miami, but Argentina's resilience and never-say-die attitude saw them through. Leer más

items.title: Morocco's top cardinal steps back amid sexual assault allegations
items.description: The Archbishop of Rabat, Cristóbal López Romero, is stepping back from his duties after at least five women accused him of sexual assault, according to an investigation by the AFP news agency.

items.title: Landslip kills seven children, teacher at Rohingya camp in Bangladesh
items.description: United Council of Rohingya president Sayed Ullah said the landslip buried an Islamic seminary inside the hilly camp complex under mud and debris during the study hours after days of heavy downpour

items.title: SAP to make it easier for customers to switch, averts EU fine
